title: Bug: evaluate accuracy --version can silently run champion when requested version config is missing

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Repro on 2026-04-03 in local runtime validation for scorecard 1039 / score 45425. Command used explicit --version 59964640-6d7e-4ba3-83ed-84c385706fa1 with --use-score-associated-dataset and baseline override. Logs show: VERSION OVERRIDE acknowledged, then 'No configuration found for version: 59964640-6d7e-4ba3-83ed-84c385706fa1', then evaluation continued and persisted scoreVersionId=db3df9e6-2f78-4f0a-9876-48bd16515cdd (champion). This is a correctness bug and violates one-path/no-fallback architecture. Expected behavior: fail fast when explicit --version config cannot be loaded; do not continue with champion/disk config.

comments.author: ryan
comments.text: Implemented fix: removed disk backfill behavior when use_cache=False in fetch_score_configurations. New contract is fail-fast if any requested version cannot be fetched from API. Added unit test: plexus/tests/test_fetch_score_configurations.py::test_fetch_score_configurations_fails_fast_when_version_missing_without_cache. Validation: reran failing command with --version 59964640-6d7e-4ba3-83ed-84c385706fa1 and confirmed non-zero exit with explicit ValueError instead of silently continuing with champion.
